debug_info.goal:
U : Type
A B : Set U
h1 : B ⊆ A
h2 : ctble ↑A
⊢ ctble ↑B

failed_attempts:
by
  have h3 : ctble (↥B) := by
    apply Set.countable_coe_iff.mpr
    simpa [Set.countable_def] using h2.mono (by exact Set.subset_univ B)
  exact Set.countable_coe_iff.mp h3

by
  have h3 : ctble B :=
    ctble.mono (by simpa using h1) h2
  exact h3

by
  have h3 : ctble A := h2
  have h4 : B ⊆ A := h1
  -- Use the fact that a subset of a countable set is countable
  have h5 : ctble B := by
    -- Use the property that a subset of a countable set is countable
    have h6 : ctble B := by
      -- Since A is countable and B is a subset of A, B is countable
      apply countable.mono h4
      exact h3
    exact h6
  exact h5

by
  have h3 : B ⊆ A := h1
  have h4 : ctble A := h2
  have h5 : ctble B := by
    apply ctble.mono
    · exact h3
    · exact h4
  exact h5

by
  have h3 : ctble B := by
    -- Since B is a subset of A and A is countable, B is also countable.
    exact ctble.mono (by simpa using h1) h2
  exact h3

by
  have h3 : ∃ (f : B → ℕ), Function.Injective f := by
    -- Since A is countable, there exists an injective function from A to ℕ.
    have h4 : ∃ (f : A → ℕ), Function.Injective f := h2
    -- Obtain such a function f : A → ℕ.
    obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h4
    -- Define the inclusion map i : B → A.
    -- This map is injective because B is a subset of A and elements are distinct.
    -- Compose f with i to get an injective function from B to ℕ.
    refine' ⟨fun x => f ⟨x.val, _⟩, _⟩
    · -- Prove that x.val ∈ A using h1 : B ⊆ A.
      have h5 : x.val ∈ B := x.prop
      have h6 : x.val ∈ A := h1 h5
      exact h6
    · -- Prove that the composition is injective.
      intro x y h7
      -- If f(i(x)) = f(i(y)), then i(x) = i(y) because f is injective.
      -- But i(x) = i(y) implies x = y because i is injective.
      have h8 : f ⟨x.val, _⟩ = f ⟨y.val, _⟩ := h7
      have h9 : ⟨x.val, _⟩ = ⟨y.val, _⟩ := hf h8
      -- Simplify the equality of subtypes to get x.val = y.val.
      have h10 : x.val = y.val := by
        simp [Subtype.ext_iff] at h9 ⊢
        <;> aesop
      -- Since x and y are elements of B with the same value, they are equal.
      have h11 : x = y := by
        apply Subtype.ext
        exact h10
      exact h11
  
  -- Use the existence of an injective function from B to ℕ to conclude that B is countable.
  exact h3

by
  have h3 : Set.Countable A := h2
  have h4 : Set.Countable B := Set.Countable.mono h1 h3
  exact h4

by
  have h3 : ctble (B : Set U) := by
    -- Use the fact that B is a subset of A and A is countable to show B is countable

      -- Use the countable set equivalence to ℕ

          -- Use the countable set equivalence to ℕ

          have h12 : Nonempty (B ≃ ℕ) := by
            -- Use the countable set equivalence to ℕ

            -- Use the countable set equivalence to ℕ

          exact h12
        exact h10
      exact h8
    exact h7
